notes: **اللّٰه** comes from the root **أ ل ه** (alif lām hā). Every time you say *Lā ilāha illallāh*, you are using this word. At its core, this root carries the idea of **worship, devotion, and turning to something with complete love and awe.** The word **إِلَٰه** is anything that your heart turns to as ultimate — anything you worship, depend on, or are deeply attached to. For some of us, it might be our car, our phone, spouse or even business or job. The Qur'an uses it when speaking about the One true God, and when challenging the false ones. - **إِلَٰه** means *a god* — one god. - When the Qur'an talks about the idols people carved and worshipped, it uses **آلِهَة** — *gods*, many of them. - And when you call upon Allah directly in your duʿā, you say **اللَّهُمَّ** — *O Allah* — the most intimate way to address your Creator.
